# Setup Instructions: Case Escalation on Inactivity
**Generated by CSAgent | 2026-04-23**
**Design Doc:** designs/2026-04-23-case-escalation.md
**Story File:** stories/2026-04-23-case-escalation.md

---

## Implementation Order

Complete these activities in the sequence below. Each step depends on the one before it.

| # | Activity | Why This Order |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Business Hours | Must exist before Escalation Rule if non-calendar SLA is required |
| 2 | Email Template: CS_CaseEscalationNotification | Must exist before Escalation Rule action references it |
| 3 | Escalation Rule: 48-Hour Inactivity Escalation | References Business Hours record and Email Template |
| 4 | List View: Stalled_Cases_48h | Deploy via SFDX — no Setup UI dependency on the above |
| 5 | Custom Report Type / Report: CS_StalledCasesReport | Requires Cases data to be present for validation |
| 6 | Dashboard: CS_SupportManagerDashboard | Requires CS_StalledCasesReport to exist |

---

## Activity 1: Business Hours — Support Business Hours

> **Note:** This step is optional. If your org requires calendar-time SLAs (24/7), skip this step and choose "Ignore Business Hours" when configuring the Escalation Action in Activity 3. Only complete this step if the 48-hour inactivity window should exclude nights, weekends, or holidays.

**Setup Path:** Setup → Company Settings → Business Hours → New

### Steps

1. In the **Business Hours Name** field enter: `Support Business Hours`
2. Set the **Default Business Hours** toggle to Off (unless this should be the org default).
3. For each working day (typically Monday through Friday), check the **Active** checkbox for that day.
4. Set the **Start Time** and **End Time** for each active day to match the support team's operational hours (e.g., 08:00 AM to 06:00 PM).
5. For Saturday and Sunday, leave the **Active** checkbox unchecked (unless weekend support is in scope).
6. In the **Time Zone** field, select the time zone of the primary support team.
7. Click **Save**.
8. Confirm the record appears in the Business Hours list before proceeding to Activity 3.

### Key Values from Design

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Business Hours Name | Support Business Hours |
| Typical Active Days | Monday – Friday |
| Usage in Escalation Rule | Attach to Escalation Action if non-calendar SLA; leave blank if 48-hour window runs 24/7 |

### Notes

- If Business Hours is not configured here, select "Ignore Business Hours" in the Escalation Action (Activity 3, Step 11) so the 48-hour clock runs continuously.
- The List View uses LAST_N_DAYS:2 (calendar days), which will differ from business-hours-based escalation timing. Communicate this distinction to support managers.
- Business Hours records cannot be deployed via `sf project deploy start` — this is a Setup UI-only step.

---

## Activity 2: Email Template — CS_CaseEscalationNotification

**Setup Path:** Setup → Classic Email Templates (search "Classic Email Templates" in Quick Find) → New Template

> **Important:** Lightning Email Templates are NOT supported by Escalation Rule notification actions as of API v62.0. You must use a Classic Email Template (Text or HTML without Letterhead). Do not use the Lightning Experience "Email Templates" path for this step.

### Steps

1. On the Classic Email Templates list page, click **New Template**.
2. On the "Select a template type" step, select **HTML (using Classic Letterhead)** or **Custom (without Letterhead)** — choose **Custom (without Letterhead)** if no branded letterhead is available, as this gives the most control over the HTML body.
3. Click **Next**.
4. Fill in the template properties:
   - **Folder:** Select an existing shared folder such as "Unfiled Public Classic Email Templates", or create a dedicated folder named `CS Templates` (Setup → Classic Email Templates → New Folder → Name: CS Templates → Save, then return to create the template).
   - **Available For Use:** Check this checkbox.
   - **Email Template Name:** `CS_CaseEscalationNotification`
   - **Template Unique Name:** `CS_CaseEscalationNotification` (auto-populated; confirm it matches exactly)
   - **Encoding:** `Unicode (UTF-8)`
   - **Description:** `Generated by CSAgent | 2026-04-23 | Escalation notification email for Cases stalled for 48+ hours with no update`
5. Click **Next**.
6. In the **Email Subject** field enter exactly:
   ```
   Case Escalation Alert: Case {!Case.CaseNumber} — No Update for 48 Hours
   ```
7. In the **Email Body (HTML)** section, enter the following body content. Replace the placeholder prose with your org's tone as needed, but preserve all merge fields exactly as shown:

   ```html
   <p>Hello,</p>

   <p>The following Case has had no update for 48 hours and has been automatically escalated to your attention.</p>

   <table>
     <tr><td><strong>Case Number:</strong></td><td>{!Case.CaseNumber}</td></tr>
     <tr><td><strong>Subject:</strong></td><td>{!Case.Subject}</td></tr>
     <tr><td><strong>Account:</strong></td><td>{!Case.Account.Name}</td></tr>
     <tr><td><strong>Contact:</strong></td><td>{!Case.Contact.Name}</td></tr>
     <tr><td><strong>Current Status:</strong></td><td>{!Case.Status}</td></tr>
     <tr><td><strong>Last Modified:</strong></td><td>{!Case.LastModifiedDate}</td></tr>
   </table>

   <p><a href="{!Case.Link}">View Case in Salesforce</a></p>

   <p>Please review this Case and take appropriate action.</p>

   <p>This notification was generated automatically by the 48-Hour Inactivity Escalation Rule.</p>
   ```

8. In the **Email Body (Text)** field (required for non-HTML fallback), enter:

   ```
   Case Escalation Alert

   The following Case has had no update for 48 hours and has been automatically escalated.

   Case Number: {!Case.CaseNumber}
   Subject: {!Case.Subject}
   Account: {!Case.Account.Name}
   Contact: {!Case.Contact.Name}
   Current Status: {!Case.Status}
   Last Modified: {!Case.LastModifiedDate}
   Case Link: {!Case.Link}

   Please review this Case and take appropriate action.

   This notification was generated automatically by the 48-Hour Inactivity Escalation Rule.
   ```

9. Click **Save**.
10. On the template detail page, click **Send Test and Verify Merge Fields**.
    - Select a real Case record from the org to populate the merge fields.
    - Confirm all seven merge fields resolve correctly (CaseNumber, Subject, Account.Name, Contact.Name, Status, LastModifiedDate, Link).
    - If any merge field displays as blank, verify the selected test Case has data in that field.
11. Note the **Template Unique Name** (`CS_CaseEscalationNotification`) — you will need it when configuring the Escalation Action in Activity 3.

### Key Values from Design

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Template Name | CS_CaseEscalationNotification |
| Template Unique Name | CS_CaseEscalationNotification |
| Template Type | Classic — Custom (without Letterhead) or Classic HTML |
| Subject | `Case Escalation Alert: Case {!Case.CaseNumber} — No Update for 48 Hours` |
| Body merge fields | `{!Case.CaseNumber}`, `{!Case.Subject}`, `{!Case.Account.Name}`, `{!Case.Contact.Name}`, `{!Case.Status}`, `{!Case.LastModifiedDate}`, `{!Case.Link}` |

### Notes

- `{!Case.Link}` renders as the full direct URL to the Case record. In Classic it is a hyperlink; in HTML email clients it is displayed as the raw URL unless wrapped in an anchor tag as shown above.
- `{!Case.Account.Name}` and `{!Case.Contact.Name}` will render as blank if the Case has no Account or Contact associated. This is expected behavior — no error is thrown.
- Classic Email Templates cannot be deployed via `sf project deploy start` — this is a Setup UI-only step.
- This template must exist and be marked "Available For Use" before the Escalation Action can reference it in Activity 3.

---

## Activity 3: Escalation Rule — 48-Hour Inactivity Escalation

**Setup Path:** Setup → Service → Escalation Rules → New

> **Pre-requisite check:** Confirm Activity 2 (CS_CaseEscalationNotification email template) is complete before starting this activity. Optionally confirm Activity 1 (Business Hours) if business-hours-based counting is required.

### Steps

**Part A — Create the Rule**

1. On the Escalation Rules list page, click **New**.
2. In the **Rule Name** field enter: `48-Hour Inactivity Escalation`
3. Check the **Active** checkbox — this deactivates any currently active Escalation Rule. Confirm with your team before activating if another rule is currently active.
4. Click **Save**.

**Part B — Add the Rule Entry**

5. On the Rule detail page, in the **Rule Entries** related list, click **New**.
6. Set **Sort Order** to `1`.
7. Under **Criteria**, set the entry criteria:
   - Select **Run this entry criteria if**: `criteria are met`
   - Field: `Case: Status`
   - Operator: `equals`
   - Value: `Open`
   - If the business also wants to escalate Cases with Status = "New" or "Waiting on Customer", click **Add Row** and add additional criteria rows for those statuses, using **OR** logic.
8. Under **Escalation Time Trigger**, set:
   - **Age Over:** `48` hours
   - **When should the time trigger fire based on?:** Select `Last Modified Date` (this ensures the 48-hour clock resets each time the Case is updated, satisfying the "stalled" definition).
9. If Business Hours should be respected (Activity 1 completed): in the **Business Hours** picklist, select `Support Business Hours`. If 24/7 calendar-time counting is required, select `Ignore Business Hours`.
10. Click **Save**.

**Part C — Add the Escalation Action**

11. On the Rule Entry detail page, in the **Escalation Actions** related list, click **New**.
12. Set **Age Over (Hours):** `48`
13. Under **Notify**, configure the notification recipients:
    - Check **Notify Case Owner's Manager** — this uses the Role Hierarchy to resolve the manager from the Case Owner's User record.
    - Optionally, if a CC distribution list is required (e.g., support-ops alias): check **Notify Other Users** and add the user(s) or enter an email address in the **Additional Emails** field.
14. Under **Email Template**, click the lookup and search for `CS_CaseEscalationNotification`. Select it.
15. Under **Reassign Case To** (optional): if the design requires automatic reassignment, select a **User** or **Queue** to reassign the Case to. Leave blank if notification-only is required (confirm with business).
16. Click **Save**.
17. Navigate back to the Rule detail page (Setup → Service → Escalation Rules → 48-Hour Inactivity Escalation) and confirm:
    - Rule Status shows **Active**.
    - One Rule Entry is listed with Sort Order 1, Criteria: Status equals Open.
    - One Escalation Action is listed under that Rule Entry showing 48 hours and the CS_CaseEscalationNotification template.

### Key Values from Design

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Rule Name | 48-Hour Inactivity Escalation |
| Active | Yes |
| Entry Sort Order | 1 |
| Entry Criteria | Case: Status equals Open |
| Age Over | 48 hours |
| Time Trigger Based On | Last Modified Date |
| Business Hours | Support Business Hours (if non-calendar SLA) or Ignore Business Hours |
| Notify | Case Owner's Manager (via Role Hierarchy) |
| Email Template | CS_CaseEscalationNotification |
| Reassign To | Confirm with business — leave blank for notification-only |

### Notes

- **Queue Owner edge case:** If a Case is owned by a Queue (not a User), Salesforce cannot walk the Role Hierarchy and the manager notification will not fire. The business must define a fallback recipient (e.g., a named manager User or the Queue's email address) before go-live. Configure this as an additional recipient in the "Additional Emails" field of the Escalation Action for Queue-owned Cases, or use an Assignment Rule to ensure Cases are assigned to Users before the escalation window opens.
- **Role Hierarchy completeness:** Audit all support agent User records (Setup → Users) to confirm each has a Role assigned. If a User's Role field is blank, the "Notify Case Owner's Manager" action will not resolve a recipient and no email will be sent for that agent's Cases.
- **Only one active rule at a time:** Activating this rule deactivates any other currently active Escalation Rule on the Case object. If an existing rule is active, review it first.
- **Escalation fires once per Case per entry:** If the Case is updated (resetting LastModifiedDate) and then stalls again, the clock resets and the escalation fires again on the next 48-hour stall. This is correct and expected behavior.
- **Sandbox testing:** To test without waiting 48 hours, either (a) shorten the Age Over to 1 hour temporarily, or (b) use Data Loader to set a Case's LastModifiedDate to a date 3+ days in the past (requires the "Set Audit Fields upon Record Creation" org permission).

---

## Activity 4: List View — Stalled_Cases_48h (Deploy via SFDX)

**Setup Path:** This List View is deployable as metadata. Use the SFDX command below rather than the Setup UI.

> This activity does not use the Setup UI. The List View metadata file is deployed directly to the org via Salesforce CLI.

### Steps

1. Confirm the file `force-app/main/default/objects/Case/listViews/Stalled_Cases_48h.listView-meta.xml` exists in your project. If it was generated by CSAgent, it should already be present.
2. Open a terminal in the project root (`e:/AIapps/CSAgent/`).
3. Run the deployment command:
   ```
   sf project deploy start --source-dir force-app/main/default/objects/Case/listViews/Stalled_Cases_48h.listView-meta.xml --target-org csagent-dev
   ```
4. After deployment completes, navigate to the Cases tab in Salesforce (App Launcher → Cases or Service Console).
5. Click the List View picklist (top-left of the Cases list) and confirm **Stalled Cases (48h+)** appears in the list.
6. Click the list view and verify:
   - Columns shown: Case Number, Subject, Account Name, Status, Case Owner, Last Modified Date.
   - Only Open Cases with a Last Modified Date older than 2 calendar days are displayed.
7. Confirm sharing: click the gear icon → Sharing Settings on the list view and verify it is shared with **All Internal Users** (or the configured profile subset per business decision).

### Key Values from Design

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| List View Name | Stalled Cases (48h+) |
| API Name | Stalled_Cases_48h |
| Object | Case |
| Filter: Status | equals Open |
| Filter: Last Modified Date | less than LAST_N_DAYS:2 |
| Columns | CaseNumber, Subject, Account.Name, Status, Owner.Name, LastModifiedDate |
| Visibility | All Internal Users (or scoped per business decision) |

### Notes

- LAST_N_DAYS:2 counts calendar days from midnight, not rolling 48 hours. A Case modified at 11:59 PM two days ago may appear in this view even though less than 48 hours have passed. This is a known platform limitation of List View relative date filters. The Escalation Rule (which counts exact hours from Last Modified Date) is the authoritative escalation mechanism.
- If the deployment fails with a "duplicate value" or "already exists" error, the list view may have been created manually. Delete the manual version via the Setup UI (Object Manager → Case → List Views) before re-running the deploy.

---

## Activity 5: Report — CS_StalledCasesReport

**Setup Path:** App Launcher → Reports → New Report

> Reports cannot be deployed via `sf project deploy start` in most orgs without special configuration. This is a Setup UI-only step.

### Steps

1. Navigate to the **Reports** tab (App Launcher → Reports).
2. Click **New Report**.
3. In the Report Type selection dialog, search for and select **Cases** (under the "Customer Support Reports" category). Click **Continue** (or **Start Report** depending on your org's UI version).
4. The Report Builder opens. In the report editor:
   - **Report Name (top of page):** `CS_StalledCasesReport`
   - **Description:** `Generated by CSAgent | 2026-04-23 | Stalled open Cases with no update for 48+ hours, grouped by Case Owner`
5. Click the **Filters** section and add the following filters:
   - Filter 1:
     - Field: `Status`
     - Operator: `equals`
     - Value: `Open`
   - Filter 2:
     - Field: `Last Modified Date`
     - Operator: `less than`
     - Value: `LAST N DAYS` → enter `2` in the days field (or select "2 Days Ago" from the relative date picker)
6. In the **Outline** section (columns/groupings):
   - Set **Group Rows By:** `Case Owner` (this produces a summary count per agent for the dashboard component).
   - Add the following columns: `Case Number`, `Subject`, `Account Name`, `Status`, `Last Modified Date`.
7. In the **Report Format** selector, confirm the format is set to **Summary** (required for grouping and for dashboard components).
8. Click **Run** to preview the report. Verify:
   - Only Open Cases appear.
   - Cases modified within the last 2 days are excluded.
   - Cases are grouped by Case Owner with a count per owner.
9. Click **Save & Run**.
10. In the **Save Report** dialog:
    - **Report Name:** `CS_StalledCasesReport`
    - **Report Unique Name:** `CS_StalledCasesReport`
    - **Report Description:** as entered in Step 4.
    - **Report Folder:** Select a shared folder accessible to Support Managers (e.g., "Support Reports" — create this folder under Reports if it does not exist: Reports → New Folder → Folder Name: Support Reports → Save).
11. Click **Save**.

### Key Values from Design

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Report Name | CS_StalledCasesReport |
| Report Type | Cases |
| Filter 1 | Status equals Open |
| Filter 2 | Last Modified Date less than LAST N DAYS 2 |
| Grouping | Case Owner |
| Format | Summary |
| Columns | Case Number, Subject, Account Name, Status, Last Modified Date |
| Folder | Support Reports (shared with Support Manager role) |

### Notes

- The report folder must be shared with the Support Manager role (or profile) so that managers can view the report and so the dashboard component can be added by a manager user in Activity 6. To share the folder: Reports → navigate to "Support Reports" folder → click the down-arrow next to it → Share → add the Support Manager role with Viewer or Editor access.
- This report is used as the data source for the dashboard component in Activity 6. Do not delete or rename the report after creating the dashboard.
- If the org uses Enhanced Analytics (CRM Analytics / Tableau CRM), verify this standard report still meets the requirement or whether a CRM Analytics dataset is preferred.

---

## Activity 6: Dashboard — CS_SupportManagerDashboard

**Setup Path:** App Launcher → Dashboards → New Dashboard

> Dashboards cannot be deployed via `sf project deploy start` in most orgs. This is a Setup UI-only step. If an existing manager dashboard already exists, consider adding the component to that dashboard instead of creating a new one.

### Steps

**Option A — Create a new dashboard:**

1. Navigate to the **Dashboards** tab (App Launcher → Dashboards).
2. Click **New Dashboard**.
3. In the **New Dashboard** dialog:
   - **Name:** `CS_SupportManagerDashboard`
   - **Description:** `Generated by CSAgent | 2026-04-23 | Stalled Cases visibility dashboard for Support Managers`
   - **Folder:** Select or create a folder shared with the Support Manager role (e.g., "Support Dashboards"). To create: Dashboards → New Folder → Folder Name: Support Dashboards → Save.
4. Click **Create**.

**Option B — Add to an existing manager dashboard:**

1. Navigate to the existing manager dashboard.
2. Click **Edit** to open the Dashboard Builder.
3. Proceed from Step 5 below.

**Add the Stalled Cases component (both options continue here):**

5. In the Dashboard Builder, click the **+ Component** button (or drag a component widget onto the canvas).
6. In the **Select Report** dialog, search for and select `CS_StalledCasesReport`. Click **Select**.
7. In the **Component Editor** panel on the right:
   - **Component Type:** Select **Vertical Bar Chart** (for visual comparison across owners) or **Summary Table** (for a precise count per agent — confirm preference with business).
   - **Title:** `Stalled Cases by Owner`
   - **X-Axis / Grouping:** `Case Owner` (should be pre-populated from the report grouping).
   - **Y-Axis / Value:** `Record Count` (count of Cases per owner).
   - **Sort By:** Record Count, descending (shows the most-stalled agents first).
   - **Maximum Values Displayed:** leave at default (10 or 20) or set based on team size.
8. Click **Add** (or **Update** if editing an existing component).
9. Resize and reposition the component on the dashboard canvas as needed.
10. Click **Save** (top-right of Dashboard Builder).
11. Click **Done** to exit the builder.
12. On the Dashboard detail page, confirm the component renders and displays Case counts grouped by owner.
13. Configure dashboard visibility:
    - Click the dashboard settings gear icon → **Share**.
    - Add the **Support Manager** role with **Viewer** access (or **Editor** if managers should be able to modify the dashboard).
    - Click **Save**.
14. Set the **Running User** for the dashboard:
    - Click the settings gear → **Edit**.
    - Under **View Dashboard As**, select **Run as logged-in user** (recommended so each manager sees only Cases they have access to) or select a specific admin user if a unified view across all Cases is required.
    - Click **Save**.

### Key Values from Design

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Dashboard Name | CS_SupportManagerDashboard |
| Component Type | Vertical Bar Chart or Summary Table |
| Component Title | Stalled Cases by Owner |
| Source Report | CS_StalledCasesReport |
| X-Axis / Grouping | Case Owner |
| Y-Axis / Value | Record Count |
| Dashboard Visibility | Support Manager role |
| Running User | Run as logged-in user (or specific admin for unified view) |

### Notes

- If the dashboard is set to **Run as a specific user** (a single admin), all managers will see all stalled Cases regardless of their own visibility. If set to **Run as logged-in user**, each manager sees only Cases they have access to under the org's sharing model. Confirm the correct behavior with the business.
- Dashboards refresh on a schedule or on demand. To enable auto-refresh: dashboard settings → Schedule Refresh → configure frequency (daily is typical for a stalled Cases dashboard).
- The dashboard and its source report are not source-trackable via `sf project deploy start` in most org configurations. Document the dashboard configuration in this instruction file as the source of truth.

---

## Post-Implementation Validation Checklist

Work through this checklist after all six activities are complete.

| # | Check | How to Verify |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Email template renders all 7 merge fields | Send Test and Verify Merge Fields on CS_CaseEscalationNotification template |
| 2 | Escalation Rule is Active | Setup → Service → Escalation Rules — confirm green Active status |
| 3 | Rule Entry criteria correct | Rule Entry detail: Status equals Open, 48 hours, Last Modified Date |
| 4 | Manager notification wired to correct template | Escalation Action detail: CS_CaseEscalationNotification selected |
| 5 | Role Hierarchy populated for all agent Users | Setup → Users — spot-check 5 agents, confirm Role field is not blank |
| 6 | List View appears on Cases tab | Cases tab → List View picker → Stalled Cases (48h+) |
| 7 | List View shows correct columns and filters | Open a Case modified 3+ days ago with Status = Open — it should appear |
| 8 | Report CS_StalledCasesReport runs without errors | Reports tab → CS_StalledCasesReport → Run |
| 9 | Dashboard CS_SupportManagerDashboard displays data | Dashboards tab → CS_SupportManagerDashboard → Refresh |
| 10 | Dashboard shared with Support Manager role | Dashboard → Share → confirm Support Manager role is listed |

---

## Sandbox Testing: Escalation Rule End-to-End Test

Use this procedure in a sandbox org to confirm the full escalation flow before go-live.

1. **Set up a test Case:** Create or identify a Case in the sandbox with:
   - Status: Open
   - Owner: A User who has a manager in the Role Hierarchy (not a Queue)
2. **Shorten the escalation window temporarily:** Edit the Escalation Rule Entry → change Age Over to `1` hour.
3. **Set Last Modified Date to the past:** Either wait 1 hour without touching the Case, or use Data Loader to backdate the LastModifiedDate field (requires the "Set Audit Fields upon Record Creation" permission in the org profile).
4. **Wait for the Escalation Rule to fire:** Escalation Rules are processed by a background job. The job runs approximately every hour. Wait up to 2 hours after the Age Over threshold is reached.
5. **Confirm results:**
   - The Case Owner's manager (per Role Hierarchy) receives an email with the subject: `Case Escalation Alert: Case [CaseNumber] — No Update for 48 Hours`
   - All 7 merge fields in the email body are populated correctly.
   - If reassignment was configured, the Case Owner field has changed to the designated user or queue.
6. **Restore the escalation window:** Edit the Escalation Rule Entry → change Age Over back to `48` hours before promoting to production.

---

## Risks and Open Decisions

| Risk | Owner | Resolution Required Before Go-Live |
|---|---|---|
| Queue-owned Cases will not generate manager notifications via Role Hierarchy | Business / Support Ops | Define a fallback recipient (named user or queue email) and configure as additional recipient in Escalation Action |
| Users without a Role assigned will not trigger manager notifications | Salesforce Admin | Audit all support agent User records and assign Roles before activating the rule |
| Classic Email Template constraint — Lightning Templates not supported by Escalation Rules as of API v62.0 | Salesforce Admin | No action required now; re-evaluate if org migrates to Lightning-only email templates in future |
| List View (LAST_N_DAYS:2) and Escalation Rule (exact 48 hours) may surface different Cases | Support Manager | Communicate the distinction to managers — list view is supplementary, not the authoritative escalation record |
| Entitlement/Milestone overlap may cause duplicate notifications | Support Ops | Confirm with support operations team that Milestone Actions do not already send equivalent notifications |
